major: Jazz, Spontaneous Composition, and Improvisation Minor
notes: The Jazz, Spontaneous Composition, and Improvisation minor is limited to students who have sufficient performance proficiency to pass auditions for entry into the jazz ensembles. PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR Music Theory Placement Exam While MUSC 14 – Beginning Western Theory and Musicianship is the recommended starting place for students with only some prior music theory experience, students may test out of MUSC 14 by placing directly into MUSC 30A – Theory, Literature and Musicianship via the Music Theory Placement Exam. The Theory Placement Exam is offered twice per year—in fall quarter, the Tuesday before classes start; and in the beginning of either winter or spring quarter. See the Music Department website for exam details. If a student places into MUSC 30A via the exam, they are not required to take MUSC 14. MUSC 11B – Introduction to Jazz MUSC 14 – Beginning Western Theory and Musicianship MUSC 30A – Theory, Literature, and Musicianship I (see above for placement exam information) Lower-Division Theory and Improvisation One of the following options: MUSC 74 – Beginning Spontaneous Composition and Improvisation MUSC 20C – Beginning Jazz Theory OR In place of MUSC 74 or MUSC 20C, students may elect to take two quarters of the following course (which may be repeated for credit): MUSC 3 – Large Jazz Ensemble Please visit the Music Department website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz students may choose to complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and allowable credits. While a minor will appear on a student's official transcript, it will not be listed on their diploma. Pursuing a minor is a personal decision that should align with a student's educational and career goals. Students do not apply for minors when submitting their application to UC Santa Cruz. After enrolling, transfer students interested in pursuing a minor should contact the department offering the minor for guidance.
